title: QA: hook deep_merge concatenates arrays unconditionally, enabling allowlist amplification
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/hooks/merge.rs:75-90
## Violation
`deep_merge` always concatenates arrays:
```rust
match (target, source) {
    (Value::Object(target_map), Value::Object(source_map)) => { ... }
    (Value::Array(target_arr), Value::Array(source_arr)) => {
        target_arr.extend(source_arr.iter().cloned());
    }
    (target, source) => {
        *target = source.clone();
    }
}
```
This is called from `load_claude_settings` (claude_compat.rs:108-184)
which loads user-global -> project -> project-local ->
managed-enterprise settings in that order. The
`allowedTools` array (extracted at claude_compat.rs:162-170) and the
`hooks` arrays are concatenated across all layers — there is no
"replace" semantic, no per-layer ACL, and no way for an enterprise
managed-settings file to **remove** a permission granted at user level.
A user can append `allowedTools` entries the enterprise tier intended to
forbid.
## Mandated refactor
Make the merge strategy explicit per top-level key. `allowedTools` and
`disallowedTools` should be unioned-then-filtered: union of all
`allowedTools`, minus union of all `disallowedTools` from any later
(higher-priority) layer. Hook arrays should similarly support
`disabledHooks` from higher layers. Document the merge semantics in
`load_claude_settings` and add tests covering "enterprise overrides
user".
## Filed from QA review dispatch
